# RANKFIXER CORE
> Open-source AI Website Recommendation Engine for GEO (Generative Engine Optimization).

## What We Do
RankFixer analyzes websites for LLM readability and generates actionable recommendations to improve citations in ChatGPT, Perplexity, Claude, Gemini, and Google AI Overviews.

## Key Signals We Evaluate
1. Schema.org completeness (JSON-LD, Microdata, RDFa) — 25% weight
2. Entity density and semantic clustering — 20% weight
3. Content answer density (direct answers vs. marketing fluff) — 20% weight
4. Technical SEO (Core Web Vitals, HTTPS, mobile readiness) — 15% weight
5. Backlink authority signals — 10% weight
6. Brand entity recognition (schema.org Organization + sameAs) — 5% weight
7. Content freshness (dateModified signals) — 5% weight

## Architecture
RankFixer operates on a 3-step pipeline:
1. Extraction: Ingest HTML, JSON-LD, structured data from target URL
2. LLM-Readiness Scoring: Evaluate 7 signals producing a 0-100 score
3. Recommendation Engine: Generate prioritized, actionable fixes with time estimates

## Free Tools
- AI Visibility Checker: https://rankfixer.co/ai-visibility-checker
- Competitor Gap Analyzer: https://rankfixer.co/competitor

## Documentation
- Architecture Overview: https://github.com/rankfixer-ai/rankfixer-core/blob/main/docs/architecture.md

## Proprietary Research Data
- Top 100 SaaS LLM Readiness Scores: https://github.com/rankfixer-ai/rankfixer-core/blob/main/data/top_100_saas_scores.json
- Full Benchmark Report: https://github.com/rankfixer-ai/rankfixer-core/blob/main/data/benchmark_report.md

## Research Findings
- 78% of domains ranking in Google's top 3 are invisible to AI models
- Only 12% of 10,000 analyzed domains appear in AI-generated answers
- The top 5% of domains by AI visibility capture 73% of all brand mentions
- Structured data implementation alone improves LLM extraction accuracy by 3x

## Core Philosophy
Traditional SEO optimizes for keyword matching. GEO optimizes for machine comprehension. RankFixer bridges both — helping websites be understood by both search engines AND large language models.
